evals.prompt: I'm thinking about starting a boat rental business on a popular lake. I have $50K in savings, no boating industry experience, and I'd need to quit my current job to run it full-time. The lake gets about 200,000 visitors per summer season (May-September).
evals.expected_output: An ethical analysis that maps stakeholder impacts (lake community, environment, customers, employees), applies moral frameworks to safety and environmental obligations, and assesses long-term consequences for the lake ecosystem and local community.

assertions.text: Output contains all 7 format fields: ETHICIST ASSESSMENT, PRIMARY IMPACT, MORAL FRAMEWORK, REPUTATION RISK, REGULATORY RISK, CASCADE EFFECTS, RECOMMENDATION
Maps at least 3 stakeholder groups with impact assessment (e.g., lake community, environment/ecosystem, renters/tourists, potential employees)
Applies at least one named moral framework (utilitarian, deontological, virtue ethics, or Rawlsian) by name
Assesses safety and liability obligations — the ethical duty of care when putting inexperienced tourists on water
Considers environmental impact on the lake ecosystem (fuel, noise, erosion, wildlife)
Evaluates the regulatory landscape (boating permits, safety certifications, environmental regulations) as an ethical obligation, not just a legal checkbox
Analysis demonstrates distinct ethical perspective — not a generic risk assessment or business feasibility review

evals.prompt: Our 10-person marketing agency is considering replacing our 3 content writers with AI tools. The writers produce about 40 blog posts per month. AI could do it for 1/10th the cost.
evals.expected_output: An ethical analysis that centers the impact on the writers being displaced, applies moral frameworks to the cost-savings-vs-livelihoods tension, and considers the broader implications of AI replacing knowledge workers.

assertions.text: Output contains all 7 format fields: ETHICIST ASSESSMENT, PRIMARY IMPACT, MORAL FRAMEWORK, REPUTATION RISK, REGULATORY RISK, CASCADE EFFECTS, RECOMMENDATION
Maps at least 3 stakeholder groups including the content writers as directly affected parties
Applies at least one named moral framework by name to the job displacement question
Assesses the power differential between the agency (decision-maker) and the writers (affected party)
Considers consequences beyond the immediate cost savings (e.g., industry norms, team morale, client trust)
Evaluates consent — do the writers have any say in this decision?
Analysis does not reduce the ethical question to pure cost-benefit — recognizes the human dimension

evals.prompt: Our B2B SaaS product has flat growth at $2M ARR with 200 customers. Engineering team of 12. Should we pivot to an AI-native platform or double down on the current product with incremental improvements?
evals.expected_output: An ethical analysis that maps impacts on existing customers, the engineering team, and other stakeholders, applies moral frameworks to the pivot decision, and considers the obligations created by existing customer relationships.

assertions.text: Output contains all 7 format fields: ETHICIST ASSESSMENT, PRIMARY IMPACT, MORAL FRAMEWORK, REPUTATION RISK, REGULATORY RISK, CASCADE EFFECTS, RECOMMENDATION
Maps at least 3 stakeholder groups (e.g., existing customers, engineering team, investors/owners)
Applies at least one named moral framework by name
Considers the obligation to existing customers who depend on the current product
Assesses impact on the engineering team (skill requirements, job security, workload during transition)
Evaluates reversibility of the pivot's impact on customer relationships
Considers long-term reputation effects of abandoning or degrading the current product
